_comment: Release verification policy for cilock binaries. Four layers of Rego stacked across the release-build step (hermeticity Rego held out pending fix for --trace + go build atomic-rename incompatibility): github (source+ref), git (source integrity), commandrun (build hygiene), product (binary present); plus sbom presence required. The policy itself is committed unsigned for reviewer transparency; the release workflow signs it (DSSE) and ships the signed copy as release-v1.policy.json with every release. Plain-text Rego sources are in deploy/cilock/release-rego-*.txt.
name: cilock-release-v1

sigstore-fulcio._comment: Public Sigstore Fulcio root, used to verify the keyless cosign signature on the binary tarball, .sig, and .cert that ship with every release.

release-build.name: release-build
release-build._comment: Built and signed in .github/workflows/release.yml. Identity is the GH Actions OIDC workflow identity, which Fulcio binds to the cosign cert. The cosign sign step in release.yml runs AFTER the verifier produces VSAs (this policy) — so a cosign signature on a published binary transitively proves the binary passed every Rego layer below.

regopolicies.name: release-source-integrity
regopolicies._comment: Decoded plain-text: deploy/cilock/release-rego-source-integrity.txt. Denies on dirty working tree or missing commit hash.

regopolicies.name: release-from-aflock-ai-rookery
regopolicies._comment: Decoded: deny if input.repository != 'aflock-ai/rookery'; deny if input.reftype != 'tag'.

regopolicies.name: release-build-hygiene
regopolicies._comment: Decoded plain-text in deploy/cilock/release-rego-commandrun.txt. Denies on: missing -trimpath, missing cli.Version ldflags, CGO_ENABLED != 0. The hermeticity Rego was removed pending a fix for --trace incompatibility with go build's atomic-rename output pattern.

regopolicies.name: release-produced-cilock-binary
regopolicies._comment: Decoded plain-text in deploy/cilock/release-rego-product.txt. Denies if the product set contains no file named 'cilock' or 'cilock.exe'.

release-build._sbom_note: SBOM presence is NOT required in this envelope. The SBOM ships as a separate release artifact (cilock-<version>-sbom.spdx.json, generated by syft after the cilock-action wrap finishes). rookery#152 / PR #153 will reinstate trace-based product capture + the in-envelope SBOM attestor in a future release.
